lede: A look at July 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ryan Johnson (ice hockey, born 2001) and Patrice Bergeron, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Angara Airlines Flight 2311
events.desc: Angara Airlines Flight 2311 crashes on approach to Tynda Airport, killing all 48 people on board.

events.title: Boris Johnson
events.desc: Boris Johnson becomes Prime Minister of the United Kingdom after defeating Jeremy Hunt in a leadership contest, succeeding Theresa May.

events.title: Air Algérie Flight 5017
events.desc: Air Algérie Flight 5017 loses contact with air traffic controllers 50 minutes after takeoff. It was travelling between Ouagadougou, Burkina Faso and Algiers. The wreckage is later found in Mali. All 116 people on board are killed.

events.title: Quietly Confident Quartet
events.desc: The Quietly Confident Quartet of Australia wins the men's 4 x 100 metre medley relay at the Moscow Olympics, the only time the United States has not won the swimming event at Olympic level.

events.title: Watergate scandal
events.desc: Watergate scandal: The United States Supreme Court unanimously ruled that President Richard Nixon did not have the authority to withhold subpoenaed White House tapes and they order him to surrender the tapes to the Watergate special prosecutor.

events.title: Treaty of Lausanne
events.desc: The Treaty of Lausanne, settling the boundaries of modern Turkey, is signed in Switzerland by Greece, Bulgaria and other countries that fought in World War I.

events.title: Hiram Bingham III
events.desc: Hiram Bingham III re-discovers Machu Picchu, "the Lost City of the Incas".

events.title: Ottoman Empire
events.desc: The Ottoman Empire captures the city of Shkodër, putting down the Albanian Revolt of 1910.

events.title: Mary, Queen of Scots
events.desc: Mary, Queen of Scots, is forced to abdicate and be replaced by her one-year-old son James VI.

events.title: Ostrogothic Kingdom
events.desc: The Ostrogoths abandoned the Siege of Ariminum upon the arrival of the larger than expected Byzantine fleet.

births.name: Keshubhai Patel
births.line: Indian politician, tenth Chief Minister of Gujarat (died 2020)

births.name: Henrik Pontoppidan
births.line: Danish journalist and author, Nobel Prize laureate (died 1943)

deaths.text: Hulk Hogan, American professional wrestler and actor
John Atta Mills, Ghanaian lawyer and politician, President of Ghana
James Chadwick, English physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Kinga (or Cunegunda) of Poland
